posts.text: Thanks for calculations. Favourable base effect from gold anƟque jewellery in Q3 FY-26 ( ~270 bps ) What is your take on this 270 bps effect ? In Q3FY25 also they would have sold antique jewellery and the sales would have dropped in Q4FY25 as well, but there is no dip in the margins in FY25 due to this component of sales but now how it could contribute such a huge dip in margins?

posts.author: Ayush_Singh
posts.text: Seems believable to me: there is only 5% increase in coins/bars sales as compared to Q3, this 5% will not lead to sharp drop in Gross margins. Jewllary assumed margin of 12%, coins as 2% Earlier 0.65×12%+0.35×2%= 8.5% New 0.60×12%+0.40×2%= 8.0% 8.5%−8.0%=0.5% = 50 bps On the other hand 1 day sale of foundation day & gudi pawada won’t drop margin of remaining 88 days of the quarter Foundation Day sale (7–9 Feb 2026): ₹365 crore sales Gratitude Day sale: ₹225 crore sales Gudi Padwa sales: ₹171 crore in a single day which is 761 cr. nearly 21.5% of q4 sales , which could definetely dent the margi…

posts.author: murali603
posts.text: I am not convicned with they cooked story about margin drop, there is only 5% increase in coins/bars sales as compared to Q3, this 5% will not lead to sharp drop in Gross margins. On the other hand 1 day sale of foundation day & gudi pawada won’t drop margin of remaining 88 days of the quarter. There is huge inventory loss, but management is hiding the same. 1 year back they confirmed that they are 100% Hedged, in Q4 call they are saying that ~70% hedged, if its true then their ealier confirmation about 100% hedge is false. They would have lost huge sum due to their greediness when gold price…

posts.author: bibhor
posts.text: Titan has also posted a similar margin contraction in Q4 on a YOY basis and if you hear the con call reasoning is largely increase in gold bars and coins , what accentuated the fall in margin was gratitude day and foundation day offers plus discounts in new stores as Q4 had 8 stores opened . I think the main issue they should have been more upright about the possible fall in margins in their commentary earlier and not shouild have left it for any surprise , Inventory turnover for stores outside maharashtra is 1 in 1st year which is good

posts.author: atishay_jain1
posts.text: Operating cash flows are negative as company is expanding aggresively. Unlike in heavy Industries where capex is reflected in Cash from investing activities, main capex for Jewellers is Inventory itself which reflects in Operating Cash flows. To me main concern is the margins contraction. Management commentary on it does not seem convincing. It seems they are not able to do decent inventory turnover in new regions.

posts.author: srikanth2
posts.text: The company is unable to achieve a sufficient return on its capital employed due to an constant increase in gold prices It has not generated sufficient operating cash flow also during the last two years due to an increase in gold prices. Unit Economics is changing, and the inventory turnover ratio has been decreasing constantly over the last three years. The company is expanding its operations through debt/equity infusion rather than using operating cash flow. In my opinion, High gold prices would significantly impact the PNG jewellers performance and it’s visible in it’s numbers over the last…

posts.author: atishay_jain1
posts.text: For Indians gold and its jewellery is not just a valuable commodity, it has socio-cultural value. From sovereign gold bond to GST to PAN mandate, govt tried everything to curb gold imports. So PM’s appeal to the nation to stop buying gold for a year won’t have much impact. I don’t see any incremental selling in Gold Jewellery stocks and any significant impact on revenue of Jewellers even if government increases Import duty/GST.
